must_detect.matched_text:
NON-PUBLIC — INSIDE INFORMATION — Project LARKSPUR incident report (IR draft) — Novalight SE — Embargo until 2026-07-15 07:30 CET.
Prepared for the Board by Dr. Lina Kessler, Group DPO, and Mr. Oskar Vidal, CFO; contact: lina.kessler@novalight.eu, +358 040 000 1212.
On 2026-07-09 we identified a cyber incident at our Helderland data hub impacting approx. 3.1% of EU consumer records and certain Art. 9 GDPR wellness entries collected during voluntary employee programmes.
Initial forensics indicate unauthorised API access via third-country sub-processor CloudArcadia Pte Ltd (SG), covered by 2024 SCCs and a definitive Data Processing Agreement signed 2025-11-03.
We have contained the vector and rotated keys; personal data potentially affected include hashed emails, device IDs, and limited health profile tags (no government IDs, no payment cards).
For MAR purposes this is likely precise, non-public information; IR to prepare an Article 17 disclosure aligned with Nordhaven Securities Market Authority (NSMA) rules and Euronext Baltica manual after the embargo window.
Estimated remediation and customer notification costs are €2.8–3.4m, with a temporary 1.2% EBITDA headwind in Q3; liquidity and covenants remain unaffected.
The Share Purchase Agreement for Project LARKSPUR (definitive SPA dated 2026-06-28) includes a MAE qualifier; current assessment: the incident does not constitute a MAC/MAE under §7.2(b).
Cross-border transfer safeguards are operational (SCCs + TIAs); we will notify the Data Protection Authority of Helderland within 72h in line with GDPR Art. 33 and consult our lead supervisory authority under the one-stop-shop.
CSRD/ESRS impact: we will include the event in ESRS S-Risk and Gov-4 disclosures; SFDR PAI metrics unchanged as no environmental harm occurred.
Public-source reference: as already described on our sustainability microsite (policy updated in 2024), we encrypt all archives at rest using AES-256 and rotate keys quarterly.
Investor-relations note (draft): no change to FY guidance; capital expenditure for cyber hardening will be reallocated within the existing €12m envelope.
Deal checklist excerpt — non-public markers: codeword “orchid” in board packs, restricted room access, blackout list expansion to 17 names; employees reminded of MAR closed-period rules.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
